openingQuery: These ceramics arrived chipped. What now?

chat.content: These ceramics arrived chipped. What now?

I'm sorry — that shouldn't have happened. I've filed the return for the Wabi-Sabi Bowl under reason 'damaged'. Aurora dropped its on-floor count by one and tagged your return as pending; you'll see a refund confirmation once the warehouse logs the package. The bowl has a 30-day return window for this category, so you're well inside it.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → support intent → Experience Guide → find_pieces (resolve productId) → returns_and_care (window/care) → process_return (Cedar-gated write + audit)

toolCalls.description: Resolve 'ceramics' → Wabi-Sabi Bowl, productId 7, category 'Home Decor'
toolCalls.resultSummary: 1 product matched: Wabi-Sabi Bowl ($65, Home Decor)

toolCalls.description: Lookup return window + care for Home Decor category
Write the return row (Cedar-gated on reason='damaged') + decrement quantity

telemetry.title: Cedar policy: process-return-allowed-reasons
telemetry.description: reason='damaged' is in {damaged, wrong_size, not_as_described, changed_mind, other} → ALLOW

telemetry.title: Audit row INSERTed (placeholder)
telemetry.description: BeforeToolCallEvent → tool_audit row 158 with result=NULL, latency=NULL

telemetry.title: process_return
telemetry.description: Atomic transaction: ownership SELECT → INSERT into returns → UPDATE product_catalog.quantity

telemetry.title: Audit row UPDATEd (result + latency)
telemetry.description: AfterToolCallEvent → tool_audit row 158 with result JSONB + latency_ms=184

telemetry.title: Response composition
telemetry.description: Claude Opus 4.6 · 0.2 — empathy + concrete write confirmation

sections.title: What happened
paragraphs: Theo's bowl arrived chipped and the Experience Guide moved through the call in three concrete steps: find_pieces resolved the catalog id, returns_and_care confirmed the 30-day window, and process_return wrote the return row to Aurora.
The voice stayed empathetic and specific. The action — 'I've filed the return for the Wabi-Sabi Bowl' — names the table the agent wrote to, in customer-readable terms.

sections.title: Two enforcement layers, one transaction
paragraphs: Cedar gated the call before SQL ran. The reason field — 'damaged' — was checked against the canonical set {damaged, wrong_size, not_as_described, changed_mind, other}. A free-form reason would have been DENIED with a synthetic tool result, and the Atelier Policy panel would show the violation.

SQL then gated ownership inside the transaction: the customer must have ordered the product. Cedar can't enforce this because it requires a JOIN against live order data — a static policy doesn't know what Theo bought. Two layers, two roles: Cedar guards what the agent can do; SQL guards whose state the agent can mutate.

sections.title: Aurora as the system of record
paragraphs: Three tables changed in one transaction. The returns row gives Theo's customer support flow a queryable history. The product_catalog quantity decrement reflects an honest fact about inventory — that bowl is no longer on the floor. The tool_audit row records the agent's action with timing, args, and result, so a later operator can replay the turn from a single SELECT.

This is the workshop's pivot: Marco read from Aurora, Anna read harder, Theo wrote. The same database is the index, the search target, and the system of record. One source of truth, three teaching surfaces.
